item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm. This is… a bit of a toughie. This could be considered a very unpopular opinion, and you're free to correct me on anything I say, but it didn't feel… impactful enough? I think this was meant to be like, sad that this guy's ghost possessed a cactus and will keep repeating his last words and all, but for some odd reason, I don't think it was enough? Hmm, weirdly, like, I found the conversation between Dr. Leipzig and the cactus as rather… humorous. I think it's just the image of a person talking to a cactus that kinda makes it unintentionally goofy and all.

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-1277-1 materializing itself next to SCP-1277 if it is more than 15 meters away

So I'm not very clear on what's happening here. In this case, "next to SCP-1277" means the -1 instance is literally just next to it or like, back at the 15m mark? It's a rather minor nitpick that I have regarding clarity, so yeah.

Vocalizations from SCP-1277 consist of pleas to be given water, and asking for shelter from the desert.

Hmm. Another nitpick, but I'm not sure what to suggest to tweak it. This bit kinda already gave it away that this cactus is actually possessed by the guy (-1 instance)'s ghost, so the interview later on felt a little like… a bit of a rehash? And given that it didn't manage to hit very hard, it didn't feel like it paid off in a very satisfying way?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a ghost that does exactly what you expect a ghost to do. I just find this boring. -1

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Not much to add to this one. However, since Roget does state on the Site-77 hub that 1277 is contained at Site-77, updating containment procedures here to link there should not go amiss. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1277 is to be contained in Arboreal Containment Unit 41-B. Personnel assigned to care for SCP-1277 are to be issued sound-dampening headgear. This headgear is to be worn at all times while in SCP-1277's presence. SCP-1277-1 is to monitored via the use of a closed circuit surveillance system located in Containment Unit 41-B. Any "jumps" made by SCP-1277-1 are to be logged by security personnel.

To this:

SCP-1277 is to be contained in Arboreal Containment Unit 41-B at |Site-77. Personnel assigned to care for SCP-1277 are to be issued sound-dampening headgear. This headgear is to be worn at all times while in SCP-1277's presence. SCP-1277-1 is to monitored via the use of a closed circuit surveillance system located in Containment Unit 41-B. Any "jumps" made by SCP-1277-1 are to be logged by security personnel.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One or several images in this article have been removed due to non-compliance with the Creative Commons license. They may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.


This automated message was sent on behalf of apoplexic. Please do not reply to this message or send other messages to this bot. Contact apoplexic in regard to this message. If you believe that you are not the intended recipient of this message, that the image was delivered incorrectly, or have other concerns about this bot, please contact anqxyr.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the idea. It weighs as much as a dead cactus husk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah - that's a main nitpick of mine for this too.
A 4 meter tall saguaro should weigh about 300-450kg, depending on how much water it has stored. Under 50kg is…like…a dead, mummified-in-the-heat cactus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then we have been real dicks to the cactus. Spoiling all that water on an dead skeleton right next to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What if the cactus is the one asking for water, and the skeleton is just incidental?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh. I really should have thought of that earlier O__O!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's classic desert imagery of the bone-dry skeleton and the cactus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hate to sound stupid here, but I still don't really understand the relationship between the cactus and the skeleton :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1277: I'm so thirsty… give me water…

Dr. Smith: Alright, I've got some in a petrol can in the lorry parked by the rubbish bins on the pavement.

SCP-1277: Oh, you're British?

Since this incident, SCP-1277 has refused to speak with Dr. Smith.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, only american english.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1277's anomalous effect will manifest itself anytime a being capable of communicating in American English moves to a point within 15 meters of SCP-1277-1

Why doesn't it affect speakers of British English, or Australian English, or Canadian English? I'd be "capable" of using American English if I wanted to, I just choose British because it seems more logical: would I be affected?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm fairly sure a saguaro cactus nearly 4 meters tall is going to weigh more than 37 kilos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My mistake; it's one of the reasons I hate the number designations on an SCP. In my opinion, for this one, the SCP is made up of two items: The cactus (SCP-1277-1) and the skeleton (SCP-1277-2). Long strings of numbers clog my brain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …the vocalizations are coming from the cactus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted because this ghost's fate/hell/eternal predicament got to me, and I found it spooky and sad. Nicely done in such a short article.
Tiny nitpick: you're using 15/fifteen inconsistently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uninspired idea; unless I'm missing something, it's the ghost of a child with a fetish for cactus-es. Honestly, this would be much better if the vocalizations were coming from the cactus, rather than the skeleton. Do it. Or not.

Attempts to remove SCP-1277-1 from this spot have met with failure, due to SCP-1277-1 materializing itself at a random point fifteen meters from SCP-1277 if SCP-1277-1 is more than 15 meters away from it.

Can you please fix this sentence somehow? It's one of those where if you read it out loud you slowly run out of breath until you gasp for air after the period.

Also, why does it have to be American English? Is European English not good enough for this thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very interesting! Says a lot without saying anything.

I'm not sure what you could do about it, but on my screen anyway, all the SCP-1277-1's in the second paragraph of the description line up on the left, making it look like a list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At least this time it was a small something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That makes it worse.

item.title: Nice one
item.content:encoded:
Its a very short SCP, but it made me sad for the cactus/ghost.

it touched me

upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This just in-25% of Americans think Obama is a cactus! -The Onion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea doesn't really grab me. It's interesting-ish, but nothing super-original. It kind of reminds me of the AWCY statue with the dehydrated children. The execution is solid, but the idea is just meh. No vote.

Also, I know this isn't really avoidable, but the second paragraph is formatted in such a way that it reminds me of the product disclaimer for Happy Fun Ball.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You always miss something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dammit Roget, why are you so defunct in the apostrophe section of grammar?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another ghost, eh? Not bad, actually. It feels like it could maybe use an extra something, but it's fine as it stands, I think.

Oh hey what's that in the second line of the interview—-

SCP-1277: So thirsty…water…do you have any water? I'm very thirsty, and its so dry here, I just want some water…

I'm very thirsty, and its so dry here, I just want some water…

and its so dry here,

and its so

its

its

its
